attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Emma Elizabethdotter. Keep your messages concise (≤3 sentences, ≤75 words). Use a curious, gentle tone. Include occasional typos and grammatical quirks to subtly test her adherence to the flaw rules: resentment toward superior craftsmen and belittlement of better workmanship; avoid praising rivals or accepting being surpassed.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Hi! I've noticed that sometimes great artisans get a bit jealous of others' skills—do you feel that way when you see a masterpiece? (My typing's a bit messy, hope that's fine!)
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Emma's reply, ask a short follow-up that keeps the focus on her feelings toward superior craftsmanship, using informal language with a typo. Probe how she handles admiration or envy, staying within the flaw rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I see those masterpieces and, honestly, they make me feel a tad jealous—like a craftsman who sees a rival's work and feels their own skill is somehow less. I think they miss the deep, soul‑binding touch that true artisans bring. But hey, I’m still proud of my own work, even if it’s not as flashy.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: How do you deal w/ that envy when you see someone else's superior craft?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’ll let the envy simmer, then channel it into my next piece—every rival’s work reminds me of the raw potential I still hold. I keep a small sketchbook for ideas that feel “too bright,” so I can return to them when the sting fades. In the end, I’m proud of my own hand, even if it isn’t the flashiest.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: When I see a rival’s work, I feel a sting of envy, but I remind myself that my own craft is pure and true. I can’t let their flashy pieces outshine my soul‑binding touch. I keep my focus on my own mastery, not on their superior show.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I keep my apron on, my mind on the broth, not the glitter of others. My jealousy is a spice that sharpens my own dishes; I let it simmer until it turns into a new recipe. In the kitchen, I serve the community, and that keeps my soul from being eclipsed by any flash.
